normalized_title: City of Fremantle Local Planning Policy 2.25 - Telecommunications
scope_summary: Guides the siting, location and design of telecommunications facilities (mobile phone towers, antennae and associated infrastructure) in the City of Fremantle that do not qualify as low-impact facilities under the Telecommunications (Low-impact Facilities) Determination 2018 and therefore require development approval. Sets qualitative requirements for co-location, preference for industrial/commercial sites, protection of heritage places and vistas, visual amenity and design integration, and zero net loss of native vegetation; explicitly defers RF health and safety matters to ARPANSA/ACMA standards.

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
City of Fremantle Local Planning Scheme No. 4
Local Planning Policy 1.3 - Community Consultation on Planning Proposals
Mobile Phone Base Station Deployment Industry Code (C564:2020)
ACMA Radiocommunications Licence Conditions (Apparatus Licence) Determination 2015

quality_flags: Originally adopted 23 July 2001 (SP34); amended 22 March 2023 (PC2303-7); next review 22 March 2027
Typos in source text: 'It Council's preference' (cl 4.1), 'Manger Strategic Planning and City Design' (responsible officer)
Policy contains qualitative siting/design criteria only; no measurable numeric development standards
Manifest instrument name says 'Telecommunications Policy'; document title is 'Local Planning Policy 2.25 Telecommunications' - same instrument
